name: handoff
description: Session continuity in a single command — /handoff detects the session state and routes. Workspace-aware: the handoff belongs to the PROJECT the session touched, not the cwd — the extractor resolves the .git boundary of the edited files, so it works for a standalone project, a monorepo (one HANDOFF-<module>.md per module, no more clobbering between parallel sessions on the same root), or an umbrella folder with nested projects. SAVE: an extractor reads the session transcript (discovered deterministically via CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID, plus teammates' transcripts when a clã/Agent Team is active) and mechanically generates a verbatim chronological ata (LOG-<sessão>.md with stable item ids), a workspace scope (project_root/module/handoff_path), AND a prospective block (open tasks + last plan) that pre-fills the 'what's left' sections; Claude then writes only the thematic PRD at scope.handoff_path referencing those ids. A Stop-hook completeness gate (keyed by session_id, finds the handoff even outside the cwd) blocks until every strong item is covered, no template placeholder ({{...}}) remains, and every non-trivial Próximos Passos step carries the 5-field molde. A handoff taken right after a completed implementation is treated as a RECORD (the executed plan goes to 'O Que Foi Feito', not 'Próximos Passos'), and RESUME reconciles against git before acting so finished work is never re-implemented. RESUME: locates the right handoff (scanning nested projects when opened from an umbrella folder), rehydrates from the PRD and actively recovers context for any thin step before acting. Optional [salvar|retomar] argument forces the mode. v1.7.0: the transcript-collection layer (schema, discovery, scope resolution, collect/anchor) moved to the shared engine `_shared/collect_engine.py`, vendored into lib/ via scripts/sync-shared.sh and shared with project-doc; handoff behavior unchanged.
